answers.body:
This is a (deliberate) JTE restriction that is apparently intended to reduce the risk of security vulnerabilities. (Whether it actually does that is open to debate ... and not really relevant to this answer.)

Is there any workaround for this?

Not really, no. Certainly not in the general case.

But what you could possibly do is to refactor the javascript so that

  @template.pages.json-ld(product = product)

can be replaced with a call to a javascript function that is defined in a static script file that is defined elsewhere.

Note that an issue has been raised for this saying that the restriction is "too opinionated"; see https://github.com/casid/jte/issues/437. You can read the developers' response for yourself.

title: Template calls in <script> blocks are not allowed
body:
I'm using the jte template engine within a Spring-Boot project and need to include a template into a <script> tag:

<script type="application/ld+json">
    @template.pages.json-ld(product = product)
</script>

Jte doesn't allow this and throws the following exception with the message Template calls in <script> blocks are not allowed.

gg.jte.TemplateException: Failed to compile pages/product.jte, error at line 16: Template calls in <script> blocks are not allowed.
    at gg.jte.compiler.java.JavaCodeGenerator.onError(JavaCodeGenerator.java:228) ~[jte-3.1.16.jar:na]
    at gg.jte.compiler.TemplateParser.doParse(TemplateParser.java:248) ~[jte-3.1.16.jar:na]
    at gg.jte.compiler.TemplateParser.parse(TemplateParser.java:85) ~[jte-3.1.16.jar:na]
    at gg.jte.compiler.java.JavaCodeGenerator$JavaContentProcessor.onContentBlock(JavaCodeGenerator.java:610) ~[jte-3.1.16.jar:na]
    at gg.jte.compiler.ContentProcessor.handleContentBlock(ContentProcessor.java:69) ~[jte-3.1.16.jar:na]
    at gg.jte.compiler.ContentProcessor.process(ContentProcessor.java:40) ~[jte-3.1.16.jar:na]
    at gg.jte.compiler.java.JavaCodeGenerator.writeJavaCodeWithContentSupport(JavaCodeGenerator.java:486) ~[jte-3.1.16.jar:na]
    at gg.jte.compiler.java.JavaCodeGenerator.appendParam(JavaCodeGenerator.java:549) ~[jte-3.1.16.jar:na]

Is there any workaround for this?
